causal_chains.description: Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed
Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex
Excess apex speed causes understeer (high lateral load)

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 1 (11.1% – 16.7% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.159s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 750.2 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed (confidence: 67%)
    💡 Tip: Get on the throttle earlier and more decisively on corner exit to maximise straight-line speed.
    → Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex (confidence: 29%)
    💡 Tip: Try initiating your braking earlier and with more initial pressure to scrub speed before the turn-in point.
zones.llm_feedback:
Here's your coaching feedback:

"Great job on the corner entry, but to improve your corner exit speed, try to apply the throttle 0.9% earlier, which will help you match the expert's speed. Additionally, aim to brake 1.2 km/h earlier to reduce your speed at the apex, allowing you to maintain a more neutral handling balance. Focus on trail-braking smoothly into the turn to set yourself up for a faster corner exit."

zones.llm_feedback:
Here's your coaching feedback:

"Great job on the braking point, you're getting close to the optimal braking point, but you're still exceeding the expert's braking by 1.6%. To improve, focus on trail-braking smoothly and gradually, aiming to match the expert's braking input. This will help you carry less speed into the apex, making it easier to navigate and reducing the need for aggressive throttle application. Remember to ease off the throttle a bit sooner, by about 0.9%, to maintain rear stability and set up a stronger corner exit."

Here's your coaching feedback:

You're entering the turn too hot, with 604 rpm above the optimal range. To improve, focus on braking earlier and more aggressively, aiming to brake at 1.0% more than the expert reconstruction. This will help you slow down before the apex, reducing the risk of understeer. As you exit the turn, ease off the throttle more gradually, avoiding aggressive trail-braking that can cause rear instability.

You're entering the braking point at 40.1% of the lap a bit too early, which is causing you to carry excess speed into the apex. To improve, try braking 1.3 km/h harder to slow down more effectively. This will help you maintain a more optimal speed through the turn and reduce the understeer you're experiencing. causal_chains.description: Braking too early causes low apex speed
Low apex speed leads to premature throttle application
Aggressive throttle application causes rear instability

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 5 (54.1% – 57.7% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.240s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 734.6 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Braking too early causes low apex speed (confidence: 100%)
    💡 Tip: You can brake later and carry more speed into the corner — trust the car's braking capability.
    → Low apex speed leads to premature throttle application (confidence: 70%)
    💡 Tip: Low mid-corner speed is tempting you to get on throttle early. Focus on carrying more apex speed instead.
zones.llm_feedback:
Here's your coaching feedback:

You're braking too early, which is causing you to miss the optimal apex speed. To improve this, try braking later by about 2.7% (compared to the expert) and aim to trail-brake more smoothly. This will allow you to carry more speed through the corner and set up a better corner exit, where you can then smoothly apply the throttle.

summary_llm: Based on the lap analysis, the single biggest area for improvement is the consistent issue of RPM undershoots, particularly in Zones 1-4, where the driver is losing up to 750.2 rpm due to late throttle application and insufficient braking. To address this, I recommend focusing on improving braking technique and applying the throttle earlier in the corner, aiming to maintain a more consistent RPM range. This will likely have a significant impact on overall lap time, potentially saving up to 0.549s. It's worth noting that the driver has already shown strong braking skills in Zone 5, where they were able to maintain a high apex speed despite braking too early.
